era_context: Identical to previous years: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI) under Pepi II, Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. This is a stable period in the development of early empires.

events.title: Egypt under Pepi II: reign continues
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues with no documented specific events for this year. The Egyptian state maintains its administrative and religious functions. This year falls within the long reign traditionally attributed to Pepi II, though the exact length and dating of his reign remain contested among Egyptologists.

sources.name: Administrative records and inscriptions from Dynasty VI

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for -2348. The year falls within Pepi II's reign based on traditional chronology, but absolute dating is uncertain.

events.title: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin: imperial apex
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues at the height of Akkadian power. The empire controls vast territories and maintains military and administrative dominance. No specific events are documented for this year.

sources.name: Akkadian royal inscriptions and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but no specific events are documented for -2348.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for -2348, making it impossible to identify disconfirming evidence.
historiographic_note: The year -2348 is not individually documented in surviving sources. Historical understanding is based on broader patterns of the era.
